title: Canary30 Re-evaluation: Chunk Parameter Impact Analysis

CHUNK_OVERLAP.description: Increased from 150 to 200 tokens (25% overlap rate)

MAX_CHUNKS_PER_MATERIAL.description: Increased from 5 to 8 chunks per document

corpus_impact.observation: Chunk count increased from 11449 to 11457 (+8 chunks, +0.07%)
corpus_impact.embedding_cache_invalidation: Required cache rebuild due to corpus size change

performance_regression.analysis: Current run shows significant regression vs aligned baseline (2026-04-27). This suggests the chunk parameter changes may have negatively impacted retrieval quality despite increased overlap and per-material limits.

hard_queries.status: POOR - No hard queries retrieved in top-5

medium_queries.status: GOOD - Strong performance on medium difficulty

simple_queries.status: WEAK - Surprisingly poor on simple queries

latency_profile.analysis: Latency increased despite no reranking, suggesting embedding computation overhead from larger corpus

root_cause_hypothesis.hypothesis: Increased chunk overlap and per-material limits may have introduced noise or diluted relevance signals
evidence: Hard queries (0% recall@5) suggest retrieval is missing complex queries entirely
Simple queries also underperform (38.89% vs expected higher), indicating systematic issue
potential_causes: Overlapping chunks may create redundancy that confuses embedding similarity scoring
Increased MAX_CHUNKS_PER_MATERIAL (5→8) may dilute top-k results with lower-relevance chunks
Embedding model may not be optimized for the new corpus size/distribution
Query expansion or contextual chunking disabled - may need re-enabling for new parameters

immediate_actions: REVERT chunk parameter changes and re-run evaluation to confirm causality
If revert improves metrics, investigate parameter tuning more carefully
Consider A/B testing: gradually increase CHUNK_OVERLAP (150→175→200) to find optimal point
investigation_steps: Run evaluation with CHUNK_OVERLAP=175 (midpoint) to isolate parameter impact
Run evaluation with MAX_CHUNKS_PER_MATERIAL=6 or 7 to find sweet spot
Enable query expansion and contextual chunking to see if they compensate for parameter changes
Analyze per-query results to identify which query types regressed most
longer_term: Implement token-aware chunking instead of character-based (current approach)
Consider dynamic chunk sizing based on document type (academic papers vs web content)
Profile embedding cache hit rates to optimize for new corpus size
Run full 109-paper evaluation to confirm canary30 findings generalize

next_action: Revert chunk parameters and re-run to confirm causality before proceeding with parameter tuning
